ops.title: The cc-notes.yml reconcile workflow is embedded in the cc-notes binary and deployed to ~25 repos that curl-install the latest release at run time — a code fix + release heals the whole fleet
ops.body:
The reconcile CI workflow template lives in the cc-notes repo itself (`plugin/workflows/cc-notes.yml`, embedded via `plugin/embed.go`, written by `cc-notes init --ci` / `cc-notes workflows install`) — NOT in cc-skills, which has no workflows at all. As of 2026-07-07 ~25 yasyf/* repos carry the deployed copy as `.github/workflows/cc-notes.yml`; every one installs the **latest release** at run time via `scripts/install.sh` (which prefers `brew install yasyf/tap/cc-notes`).

**Why:** fleet-wide workflow bugs are usually fixable with a cc-notes code change + release tag — zero per-repo workflow edits. Conversely, verification ordering matters: a rerun before the release's `bump-formula` job publishes to the tap installs the OLD binary and falsely implicates the fix ([[homebrew-distribution-cc-pool-pattern]]).

**How to apply:** for reconcile-workflow failures across repos, fix in cc-notes (`internal/lfs` / `internal/sync` / template), tag, wait for release + tap bump, then `gh run rerun` an affected repo (e.g. cc-pool) as the live test. Find affected repos with `gh api search/code` for `filename:cc-notes.yml user:yasyf`. LFS batch auth in CI works via `http.<url>.extraheader` honored since v0.20.0.
